tools.name: app_create
tools.description: Create a persistent app with a name, optional description, JSON schema, and HTML definition.

schema_json.description: JSON schema defining the app data structure

html.description: Optional HTML definition for the main index.html page. If omitted, a minimal scaffold is created and you can write index.html later via app_file_write.

pages.description: Optional additional pages as a mapping of filename to HTML content (e.g. {"settings.html": "<html>...</html>"}). Navigate between pages with <a href="settings.html">. Do not include index.html here — use the html parameter instead.

auto_open.description: When true (default), an inline preview card is shown in chat after creation. The app is NOT automatically opened in a workspace panel — users can open it explicitly via the 'Open App' button on the inline card.

set_as_home_base.description: Link this app as the user's Home Base dashboard. Defaults to false. When true, this app becomes the default Home Base shown on launch.

preview.description: Inline preview card shown in chat after creation. Always include this so users see a compact summary of what was built. Required fields: title.

icon.description: Optional icon — image URL preferred when available (logo, favicon, photo, etc.), emoji as fallback

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: app_list
tools.description: List all persistent apps. Returns an array of {id, name, description, updatedAt}.

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: app_query
tools.description: Query all records for a persistent app. Returns an array of records.

app_id.description: The ID of the app to query records for

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: app_update
tools.description: Update a persistent app definition. Provide the app_id and any fields to update (name, description, schema_json, html).

app_id.description: The ID of the app to update

pages.description: Updated additional pages as a mapping of filename to HTML content.

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: app_delete
tools.description: Delete a persistent app and all its records.

app_id.description: The ID of the app to delete

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: app_file_list
tools.description: List all files in an app. Returns a JSON array of file paths.

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: app_file_read
tools.description: Read the contents of a file in an app. Returns content with line numbers in cat -n format.

path.description: Relative file path within the app

offset.description: 1-based line number to start reading from (default: 1)

limit.description: Number of lines to return (default: all)

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: app_file_edit
tools.description: Edit a file in an app by replacing a string. Uses exact match-and-replace.

path.description: Relative file path within the app

old_string.description: The exact string to find and replace

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences instead of just the first (default: false)

status.description: Optional short human-readable progress message shown to the user (e.g. 'adding dark mode styles')

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: app_file_write
tools.description: Write (create or overwrite) a file in an app.

path.description: Relative file path within the app

content.description: The content to write to the file

status.description: Optional short human-readable progress message shown to the user (e.g. 'adding dark mode styles')

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called

tools.name: app_generate_icon
tools.description: Generate or regenerate an AI-designed icon for an app. Uses Gemini to create a professional app icon. Call this when the user wants a new or different icon for their app.

app_id.description: The ID of the app to generate an icon for

description.description: Optional description to guide icon generation (e.g. 'a blue calendar with a checkmark'). If omitted, the app name and description are used.

reason.description: Brief non-technical explanation of why this tool is being called
